texto: The patriarchal/kyriarchal/hegemonic culture seeks to regulate and control the body – especially women’s bodies, and especially black women’s bodies – because women, especially black women, are constructed as the Other, the site of resistance to the kyriarchy. Because our existence provokes fear of the Other, fear of wildness, fear of sexuality, fear of letting go – our bodies and our hair (traditionally hair is a source of magical power) must be controlled, groomed, reduced, covered, suppressed.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Yvonne Aburrow sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. A afirmação “The patriarchal/kyriarchal/hegemonic culture seeks to regulate and control the body – especially …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, body, body-image, útil em momentos de body-image e feminism.
